content.text:
The issue was caused by `parseSourcesToFileScheme` in `TemplateManager.kt` stripping `<script>` tags when parsing card templates containing media references. This happened because `Jsoup.parse(content)` was used, which interprets the content as a full HTML document. If a script tag appeared at the beginning of the content, Jsoup would place it in the `<head>`, but the function only returned `doc.body().html()`, thereby discarding the script tag.

I fixed this by changing `Jsoup.parse(content)` to `Jsoup.parseBodyFragment(content)`. This ensures that the entire input content is treated as part of the `<body>`, so `doc.body().html()` correctly returns the full content including any script tags.

I verified the fix with a reproduction test case that confirmed the script tag is preserved after parsing, and I ran the existing `TemplateManagerTest` suite to ensure no regressions were introduced.

Diff of changes:
```patch
AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/libanki/TemplateManager.kt
@@ -340,7 +340,7 @@
 @NotInLibAnki
 @VisibleForTesting
 fun parseSourcesToFileScheme(content: String, mediaDir: String): String {
-    val doc = Jsoup.parse(content)
+    val doc = Jsoup.parseBodyFragment(content)
     doc.outputSettings(OutputSettings().prettyPrint(false))
 
     fun replaceWithFileScheme(tag: String, attr: String): Boolean {
```
